response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and backend software engineer with strong web, mobile, and some low-latency/embedded experience, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator performance optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no SoC/IP modeling, no deep-learning workload optimization, no SIMD/MIMD or GPU architecture work, and no relevant performance-architecture background. While years of experience satisfy the BS+8 baseline, the core technical domain is essentially absent, making this a poor fit for a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: 13+ years of broad software engineering experience across the stack
Some low-latency, high-throughput backend work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at Dunnhumby
C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and embedded/robotics tinkering
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware optimization, or system architecture for accelerators
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture experience
Background is web/full-stack product engineering, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/DNN optimization or GPU architecture experience
